title: noInlineStyles
description: Learn more about noInlineStyles
---
import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="JavaScript (and super languages)" icon="seti:javascript">
:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noInlineStyles`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule has an [**unsafe**](/linter/#unsafe-fixes) fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noInlineStyles": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Disallow the use of inline styles.

Inline styles via the `style` attribute make code harder to maintain and override,
prevent reusability of styling, and can be a security concern when implementing
a strict Content Security Policy (CSP).

Instead of inline styles, use CSS classes, CSS modules, or a styling library.

## Examples

### Invalid

```jsx
<div style={{ color: "red" }}>Error</div>
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.jsx:1:6 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-inline-styles">lint/nursery/noInlineStyles</a> <span style="color: #000; background-color: #ddd;"> FIXABLE </span> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Unexpected </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>style</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> attribute.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>&lt;div style=&#123;&#123; ### Valid

```jsx
<div className="text-red">Error</div>
```

```js
React.createElement("div", { className: "container" });
```

- This rule has an [**unsafe**](/linter/#unsafe-fixes) fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- Sources:

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noInlineStyles": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Disallow the use of inline styles.

Inline styles via the `style` attribute make code harder to maintain and override,
prevent reusability of styling, and can be a security concern when implementing
a strict Content Security Policy (CSP).

Instead of inline styles, use CSS classes, CSS modules, or a styling library.

## Examples

### Invalid

```html
<div style="color: red;"></div>
```
